reasoning:
The reference price will be established on Jan 16, 2026, which is 81.71. 
The statistical model from yfinance gives P(exceeding 81.71 in a week) at 0.55-0.67 depending on metrics, anchored at 0.61. 
However, there is recent negative momentum caused by a UBS downgrade to "Sell" on Jan 15 (price target 77.00). The stock dropped from ~85 to ~81.7 upon this news. Downwards momentum following a downgrade can persist for several days to a few weeks, which dampens the baseline upward drift expected from stocks. 
Earnings will be announced on Wed, February 4, 2026. This means the Jan 25 resolution date precedes earnings, and the Feb 17 resolution date follows earnings. 
Given the conflicting forces: 
(1) Statistical trend and historic seasonality point to an upward mean reversion (pushing >0.5).
(2) The recent UBS downgrade and target of $77 (below current 81.71) provides a short-term bearish catalyst (pushing <0.5).
I will forecast slightly above 0.50 since stock indices generally drift upwards, the drop to 81.71 may have fully priced in the downgrade, and the market consensus remains largely a 'moderate buy'. I predict 0.53 for Jan 25. For Feb 17 (post-earnings), uncertainty increases and the horizon expands, so it should be slightly closer to 0.50 (I forecast 0.52).
